assumptions: The intragroup dividend gives rise, at the consolidated level, to a recognised foreign-currency-denominated monetary item or a foreign-currency exposure considered for hedge accounting purposes.
The question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows associated with a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
